key_insight: DeepCormack combines physics-based reconstruction with deep learning to recover 3D Fermi-surface momentum densities from sparse ACAR measurements much more accurately than classical Cormack-based methods.
engineering_relevance: It can cut ACAR acquisition times from months toward weeks while preserving reconstructions needed for practical Fermi-surface studies in materials research.
method: Start with the standard MCM reconstruction framework for symmetry-aware ACAR inversion.
Insert supervised models (CNN, MLP, or UNet) at different stages of the pipeline to refine reconstructions.
Generate synthetic TPMD training volumes from one DFT reference density using SVD + dynamic mode decomposition.
evidence_signal: On test data, DeepCormack improves reconstruction quality by about 8.5 dB PSNR at 200M counts and remains stable at lower counts.
watch_item: Performance on real samples depends strongly on how closely the DFT-derived training distribution matches the experimental material, so sample-specific training is important.
paper_title: DeepCormack: Fermi surface tomography using model-based data-driven algorithms
